message:
chore(beads): reopen flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2 (scanner-smoke integration)

Record a beads status change for issue flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2,
"[scaffolding] Integrate generator regression tests with scanning and smoke
verification":

- status: in_progress → open
- updated_at: 2026-04-03T17:52:47Z → 2026-04-19T14:13:12Z
- new comment from MagentaStream explaining the reopen:
    "stalled for 8 days with no activity, assignee unclear. Returning to
    ready queue."

No runtime code changes — this is a pure .beads/issues.jsonl persistence
flush so the reopen survives across agents and machines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","title":"[scaffolding] Redesign fwc new to emit truthful minimal viable connectors","description":"Redesign fwc new so generated connectors start from a truthful minimal viable baseline instead of dead runtime placeholders. The generator should emit a compiling connector skeleton with honest manifest metadata, explicit extension points, and no placeholder_operation, api client not implemented, or bidirectional send not implemented paths in the runtime surface.","acceptance_criteria":"- Newly generated connectors compile and handshake from a truthful minimal baseline.
- Emitted runtime code contains no placeholder_operation or equivalent dead-path placeholders.
- Generated connectors default to the explicit truthful non-live state defined by the graduation or quarantine model and emit a minimal verification recipe or script scaffold aligned to the shared evidence convention, with unit tests and end-to-end generator smoke scripts validating emitted manifests, runtime behavior, and logs.","notes":"The generator should leave new connector authors with a truthful starting point and an obvious first verification path. That means the emitted scaffold should not only avoid dead runtime placeholders; it should also point authors at the right minimal smoke checks, transcript expectations, and next implementation steps without inventing a second status model.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubySnow","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:10.773594Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:25:03.046988Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:25:03.046530Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","generator","scaffolding","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:28.159281Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:19.643944Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:48:31.221216Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:38:58.603738Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:10.773594Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2335,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"Codex","text":"Audit anchors: crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs generator templates that currently emit TODO-heavy placeholder operations and dead runtime paths. Verification contract: unit tests for emitted template shaping, integration coverage that the generated crate compiles/handshakes truthfully, and end-to-end generator smoke scripts with detailed logs proving the output defaults to the correct truthful non-live state.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:28Z"},{"id":2379,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"RubySnow","text":"Claimed this bead. Write set is currently limited to crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs. Scope: replace generated dead-path placeholder operations and not-implemented scaffold strings with a truthful minimal non-live connector baseline that can still compile, handshake, and introspect honestly; keep verification rch-only for any Cargo work.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:00:07Z"},{"id":2380,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the scaffold redesign in crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs. The generator now emits an honest incubating local-only scaffold centered on scaffold_status instead of fake placeholder/live semantics. Waiting on rch verification: cargo check -p fwc --all-targets and cargo test -p fwc new_cmd -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:17:17Z"},{"id":2383,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implementation in crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs is complete. Remote verification is currently blocked upstream: both rch exec -- cargo check -p fwc --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo test -p fwc new_cmd -- --nocapture failed in /dp/asupersync/src/http/h1/listener.rs:493 with error[E0425] cannot find function payload_to_string in this scope. No fwc-local compiler or test failure surfaced before that dependency break.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:24:36Z"},{"id":2393,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Continuing work past the blocked verification state: now investigating and fixing the upstream asupersync compiler failure that is preventing rch verification from completing for this bead.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:06:29Z"},{"id":2394,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Patched the direct asupersync path references in seven Cargo manifests from the machine-local /dp/asupersync alias to the real sibling checkout path ../../../asupersync so rch can sync current dependency source instead of relying on stale worker-local state. Re-running rch verification now.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:09:34Z"},{"id":2444,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"RubySnow","text":"Follow-on fixes landed after the original asupersync blocker: added the missing fcp-policy workspace dependency back to crates/fwc/Cargo.toml, moved test-only policy imports behind #[cfg(test)] in crates/fwc/src/policy_cmd.rs, normalized the remaining /dp/toon_rust dependency path to ../../../toon_rust, and cleaned the next surfaced fwc test warnings in crates/fwc/src/e2e_scripts.rs and crates/fwc/src/main.rs. rch verification is still in progress on the updated manifest/code state.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:25:00Z"},{"id":2445,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"RubySnow","text":"Upstream sibling check is green and the remaining work is active remote verification of fwc. The earlier external blocker is cleared; I am iterating on whatever the current remote compiler/test surface exposes next.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:36:03Z"},{"id":2448,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Remote fwc compiler verification is green again after the asupersync path fix and warning cleanup. The remaining finish-line work is validating the corrected operator-truth expectation for offline show plus a fresh lint pass on healthy worker transport.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:57:44Z"},{"id":2683,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Verified all acceptance criteria met — 164 generator tests pass, clippy clean. Generator emits truthful incubating scaffold with scaffold_status (no placeholder_operation), prechecks (10-point), next_steps verification recipe, E2E test stubs, and golden fixture. Fixed 5 remaining clippy warnings (intent.rs dead_code, supply_chain_cmd Option<&T> idiom, 3 unit pattern matches). Commits fa2c573f, ddb34d8e.","created_at":"2026-04-11T02:24:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","title":"[scaffolding] Add generator regression tests so new connectors never ship with runtime placeholders","description":"Add regression protection around the generator so placeholder output does not creep back in. Golden tests, snapshots, or comparable checks should verify both emitted code and manifests, and they should integrate with the production placeholder scanner so generated runtime code cannot silently regress to TODO-heavy dead ends.","acceptance_criteria":"- Generator regression tests fail on TODO or placeholder markers in emitted runtime code or manifests.
- The proof layer includes unit, golden, or snapshot coverage, scanner integration, and end-to-end generator smoke scripts with detailed logs and artifacts.
- Future generator template changes are forced through the truthful-runtime standard rather than reintroducing dead placeholder paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:11.386263Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:57.380826Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generator","scaffolding","tooling","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:19.900718Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:00:27.586943Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:11.386263Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:20.165652Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2336,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","author":"Codex","text":"Audit anchors: crates/fwc/src/new_cmd.rs and the broader placeholder scanner/allowlist rules. Verification contract: keep golden/snapshot unit tests for emitted files, integrate them with the production placeholder scanner, and add end-to-end generator smoke scripts with detailed logs so regressions in emitted runtime code are caught mechanically.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:40:28Z"},{"id":2388,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Split generator regression protection into file-level goldens and scanner/smoke integration. The intent is to catch both template drift and end-to-end scaffold truth regressions instead of treating all generator breakage as one undifferentiated test failure.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","title":"[scaffolding] Freeze golden fixtures for truthful generated connector output","description":"Background: once `fwc new` emits truthful scaffolds, the repo still needs a regression layer that inspects the emitted files themselves. Without golden coverage, future template edits can quietly reintroduce TODO-heavy runtime shells while the generator still appears to work.

Scope: add golden or snapshot coverage for emitted manifests, runtime code, and scaffold-status metadata from `fwc new`. Verify that the generated connector starts in the correct truthful non-live state, contains no dead runtime placeholder operations, and points authors at the expected verification entrypoints.

Acceptance: template regressions are caught by file-level golden coverage before they leak into downstream connector work, and the emitted baseline remains self-consistent across manifest metadata, runtime behavior, and author guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:46.516711Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:29:06.932554Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:29:06.932056Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generator","golden-tests","scaffolding","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:55.515408Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:46.516711Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2408,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group generator-proof note: the goldens and scan or smoke verification here need to prove that freshly generated connectors are truthful from the first scaffold. Require stable fixtures, scanner output, smoke logs, and rerun commands so future generator changes fail loudly and locally instead of reintroducing runtime placeholders by accident.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:09Z"},{"id":2535,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"Claiming this bead. Verification is currently blocked by an unrelated compile error in crates/fwc/src/intent.rs from in-progress bead flywheel_connectors-h6qe2.1.1, so the golden fixture cannot be emitted until fwc builds again.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:16:16Z"},{"id":2684,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Added golden fixture bundles for streaming and webhook archetypes (request-response already existed). Added 3 cross-archetype metadata consistency tests verifying incubating status in manifests, scaffold_status/live_requests_supported in connector.rs, and test template completeness. 7 golden/consistency tests all pass, clippy clean. Commit 0cfce1b1.","created_at":"2026-04-11T02:29:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2","title":"[scaffolding] Integrate generator regression tests with scanning and smoke verification","description":"Background: file-level goldens are necessary but not sufficient; the generator also needs to stay integrated with the placeholder scanner and a minimal smoke path so future changes cannot pass isolated tests while breaking the real workflow.

Scope: wire generator regression tests into the placeholder scanner and a minimal smoke harness that exercises scaffold creation, compile or handshake readiness, and evidence-bundle entrypoint generation. Treat this as the guardrail that keeps the repo from generating fresh placeholder debt after 24llg closes.

Acceptance: scanner and smoke-harness failures make it obvious whether the regression came from template content, scaffold-status semantics, emitted verification instructions, or downstream runtime truthfulness, and the resulting artifacts are strong enough for the final placeholder-closure audit.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:47.080689Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:29:28.665483Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generator","scaffolding","scanning","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:56.348640Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:47.080689Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:52:55.967474Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2409,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.7.4.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group generator-proof note: the goldens and scan or smoke verification here need to prove that freshly generated connectors are truthful from the first scaffold.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-254d","title":"[IDEA-14] Connector Limits Constants Generator","description":"# Connector Limits Constants Generator

## Goal
Standardize platform-specific limits (message length, embed caps, payload sizes) via a generated `limits.rs` module per connector to avoid magic numbers.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram both require strict length limits; scattered constants are easy to miss. A canonical `limits.rs` improves correctness and testability.

## Scope
- Generator support in `fcp new` and documentation.
- Optional lint to ensure connectors define limits constants.

## Deliverables
- `limits.rs` template with TODO placeholders and defaults.
- Guidance on how to update limits when APIs change.

## Tests
- Unit test in generator to ensure limits file generated.
- Connector test helper verifying limit constants used in validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New connectors have a `limits.rs` by default.
- Validation functions use limits constants, not magic numbers.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:08.279393213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514131459Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514131459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-254d","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-35ct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2653","title":"[FCP2] Operation Cancellation: Graceful Abort with Cleanup","description":"# [FCP2] Operation Cancellation: Graceful Abort with Cleanup

## Goal
Enable agents to cancel in-flight operations gracefully, with proper cleanup, partial result handling, and audit trail.

## Problem
Long-running operations (uploads, batch processing, streaming) may need to be cancelled:
- User changed their mind
- Agent detected an issue mid-operation
- Timeout approaching, want graceful abort
- Resource constraints (memory, cost)

Currently, there's no standardized way to:
- Request cancellation of a specific operation
- Propagate cancellation to connectors
- Handle partial results from cancelled operations
- Audit cancelled operations properly

## Solution: Cancellation Token Protocol

### Request Format
```jsonc
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "operations/cancel",
  "params": {
    "operation_id": "op_abc123",
    "reason": "user_requested",
    "cleanup_behavior": "best_effort",
    "return_partial": true
  }
}
```

### Cancel Reason Codes
```rust
pub enum CancelReason {
    /// User explicitly requested cancellation
    UserRequested,
    /// Agent detected issue and is aborting
    AgentAbort { reason: String },
    /// Approaching timeout, graceful shutdown
    TimeoutApproaching { remaining_ms: u64 },
    /// Resource limit approaching (memory, cost)
    ResourceLimit { resource: String, current: u64, limit: u64 },
    /// Superseded by another operation
    Superseded { by_operation_id: OperationId },
    /// Session/connection closing
    SessionClosing,
}
```

### Cleanup Behavior
```rust
pub enum CleanupBehavior {
    /// Best effort cleanup, may leave partial state
    BestEffort,
    /// Must fully clean up before returning (may take longer)
    Full { timeout: Duration },
    /// No cleanup, abandon immediately
    Abandon,
    /// Checkpoint state for potential resume
    Checkpoint,
}
```

### Response Format
```jsonc
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "result": {
    "status": "cancelled",
    "operation_id": "op_abc123",
    "cancelled_at": "2026-01-22T18:00:00Z",
    "cleanup_result": {
      "status": "completed",
      "actions_taken": ["rolled_back_transaction", "deleted_temp_files"],
      "duration_ms": 150
    },
    "partial_result": {
      "completed_items": 45,
      "total_items": 100,
      "data": [...]  // Partial data if requested
    },
    "checkpoint": {
      "id": "ckpt_xyz789",
      "resumable": true,
      "expires_at": "2026-01-22T19:00:00Z"
    }
  }
}
```

### Cancellation States
```rust
pub enum CancellationState {
    /// Cancellation requested, not yet acknowledged
    Requested { at: DateTime<Utc> },
    /// Connector acknowledged, cleanup in progress
    InProgress { acknowledged_at: DateTime<Utc> },
    /// Cancellation complete
    Completed {
        completed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
        cleanup_result: CleanupResult,
    },
    /// Cancellation failed (operation completed anyway)
    Failed { reason: String },
    /// Operation was already complete when cancel arrived
    TooLate { completed_at: DateTime<Utc> },
}
```

### Implementation

#### Host-Side Cancellation Manager
```rust
pub struct CancellationManager {
    /// Active operations that can be cancelled
    operations: DashMap<OperationId, OperationHandle>,
    /// Cancellation tokens for propagation
    tokens: DashMap<OperationId, CancellationToken>,
}

impl CancellationManager {
    /// Register an operation as cancellable
    pub fn register(&self, op_id: OperationId) -> CancellationToken {
        let token = CancellationToken::new();
        self.tokens.insert(op_id.clone(), token.clone());
        token
    }

    /// Request cancellation of an operation
    pub async fn cancel(
        &self,
        op_id: &OperationId,
        reason: CancelReason,
        behavior: CleanupBehavior,
    ) -> Result<CancellationResult, FcpError> {
        // 1. Find the operation
        let token = self.tokens.get(op_id)
            .ok_or(FcpError::operation_not_found(op_id))?;

        // 2. Signal cancellation
        token.cancel();
        tracing::info!(
            operation_id = %op_id,
            reason = ?reason,
            "Cancellation requested"
        );

        // 3. Wait for acknowledgment based on behavior
        match behavior {
            CleanupBehavior::Abandon => {
                // Don't wait, return immediately
                Ok(CancellationResult::requested())
            }
            CleanupBehavior::BestEffort => {
                // Wait up to 5 seconds for cleanup
                tokio::time::timeout(
                    Duration::from_secs(5),
                    self.wait_for_cleanup(op_id),
                ).await.unwrap_or(CancellationResult::timeout())
            }
            CleanupBehavior::Full { timeout } => {
                // Wait for full cleanup
                tokio::time::timeout(
                    timeout,
                    self.wait_for_cleanup(op_id),
                ).await?
            }
            CleanupBehavior::Checkpoint => {
                // Request checkpoint before cleanup
                self.checkpoint_and_cleanup(op_id).await
            }
        }
    }

    /// Check if operation is cancelled
    pub fn is_cancelled(&self, op_id: &OperationId) -> bool {
        self.tokens.get(op_id)
            .map(|t| t.is_cancelled())
            .unwrap_or(false)
    }
}
```

#### SDK Cancellation Support
```rust
/// Cancellation-aware tool execution
#[fcp_tool(cancellable = true)]
async fn upload_large_file(
    ctx: &ToolContext,
    input: UploadInput,
    cancel_token: CancellationToken,  // Injected by SDK
) -> Result<UploadOutput, FcpError> {
    let mut uploaded = 0u64;

    for chunk in file.chunks(CHUNK_SIZE) {
        // Check for cancellation before each chunk
        if cancel_token.is_cancelled() {
            // Cleanup: delete partial upload
            cleanup_partial_upload(&input.destination).await?;
            return Err(FcpError::cancelled("Upload cancelled by user"));
        }

        upload_chunk(chunk).await?;
        uploaded += chunk.len() as u64;
    }

    Ok(UploadOutput { bytes_uploaded: uploaded })
}

/// Cancellation token for cooperative cancellation
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct CancellationToken {
    inner: Arc<AtomicBool>,
    notify: Arc<Notify>,
}

impl CancellationToken {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            inner: Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false)),
            notify: Arc::new(Notify::new()),
        }
    }

    /// Check if cancelled (non-blocking)
    pub fn is_cancelled(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
    }

    /// Wait until cancelled
    pub async fn cancelled(&self) {
        if self.is_cancelled() {
            return;
        }
        self.notify.notified().await;
    }

    /// Signal cancellation
    pub fn cancel(&self) {
        self.inner.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
        self.notify.notify_waiters();
    }

    /// Create a child token that cancels when parent cancels
    pub fn child(&self) -> CancellationToken {
        let child = CancellationToken::new();
        let child_clone = child.clone();
        let parent = self.clone();
        tokio::spawn(async move {
            parent.cancelled().await;
            child_clone.cancel();
        });
        child
    }
}
```

#### Integration with Batch Operations
```rust
impl BatchInvokeExecutor {
    pub async fn execute_with_cancellation(
        &self,
        request: BatchInvokeRequest,
        cancel_token: CancellationToken,
    ) -> BatchInvokeResponse {
        let mut results = Vec::new();
        let mut cancelled_ops = Vec::new();

        for op in request.operations {
            // Check cancellation before each operation
            if cancel_token.is_cancelled() {
                cancelled_ops.push(OperationResult {
                    id: op.id,
                    status: "cancelled",
                    error: Some(FcpError::cancelled("Batch cancelled")),
                });
                continue;
            }

            // Execute with child token
            let child_token = cancel_token.child();
            let result = self.execute_single(op, child_token).await;
            results.push(result);
        }

        BatchInvokeResponse {
            results,
            cancelled: cancelled_ops,
            status: if cancelled_ops.is_empty() { "complete" } else { "partial" },
        }
    }
}
```

### Audit Trail for Cancellations
```rust
pub struct CancellationAuditEvent {
    pub operation_id: OperationId,
    pub reason: CancelReason,
    pub requested_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub completed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    pub cleanup_result: Option<CleanupResult>,
    pub partial_result_size: Option<u64>,
    pub agent_context: AgentContext,
}

// Logged as:
// {
//   "event": "operation.cancelled",
//   "operation_id": "op_abc123",
//   "reason": "user_requested",
//   "cleanup": {"status": "completed", "duration_ms": 150},
//   "partial_items": 45,
//   "agent_id": "agent-xyz",
//   "trace_id": "trace-789"
// }
```

### Tests

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cancellation_basic() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;

    // Start long operation
    let op_id = harness.start_slow_operation(Duration::from_secs(60)).await;

    // Cancel it
    let result = harness.cancel(&op_id, CancelReason::UserRequested, CleanupBehavior::BestEffort).await.unwrap();

    assert_eq!(result.status, "cancelled");
    assert!(result.cleanup_result.is_some());
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cancellation_with_partial_results() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;

    // Start batch operation
    let op_id = harness.start_batch_operation(100).await;

    // Wait for some progress
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500)).await;

    // Cancel with partial results
    let result = harness.cancel_with_partial(&op_id).await.unwrap();

    assert!(result.partial_result.is_some());
    assert!(result.partial_result.unwrap().completed_items > 0);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cancellation_propagates_to_connector() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;
    let connector = harness.mock_connector();

    let op_id = harness.invoke_connector(&connector, "slow_op").await;

    // Cancel
    harness.cancel(&op_id, CancelReason::UserRequested, CleanupBehavior::Full { timeout: Duration::from_secs(10) }).await.unwrap();

    // Verify connector received cancellation
    assert!(connector.received_cancellation(&op_id));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cancellation_too_late() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;

    // Start fast operation
    let op_id = harness.start_fast_operation().await;

    // Wait for completion
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;

    // Try to cancel
    let result = harness.cancel(&op_id, CancelReason::UserRequested, CleanupBehavior::BestEffort).await.unwrap();

    assert!(matches!(result.state, CancellationState::TooLate { .. }));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_cancellation_checkpoint_resume() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;

    // Start resumable operation
    let op_id = harness.start_resumable_operation(1000).await;

    // Wait for progress
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1)).await;

    // Cancel with checkpoint
    let result = harness.cancel(&op_id, CancelReason::UserRequested, CleanupBehavior::Checkpoint).await.unwrap();

    assert!(result.checkpoint.is_some());
    let checkpoint = result.checkpoint.unwrap();
    assert!(checkpoint.resumable);

    // Resume from checkpoint
    let resumed_op = harness.resume_from_checkpoint(&checkpoint.id).await.unwrap();
    let final_result = harness.wait_for_completion(&resumed_op).await.unwrap();

    assert!(final_result.success);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_batch_cancellation_stops_remaining() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;

    // Start batch with 10 operations
    let batch_id = harness.start_batch(10, Duration::from_millis(100)).await;

    // Wait for a few to complete
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(250)).await;

    // Cancel batch
    let result = harness.cancel_batch(&batch_id).await.unwrap();

    // Some completed, some cancelled
    assert!(result.completed > 0);
    assert!(result.cancelled.len() > 0);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agents can request cancellation of any in-flight operation
- [ ] Cancellation propagates to connector within 100ms
- [ ] Connectors can perform cleanup before returning
- [ ] Partial results are available on request
- [ ] Checkpoint/resume is supported for long operations
- [ ] Batch cancellation stops remaining operations
- [ ] Audit trail includes cancellation events
- [ ] "Too late" cancellation returns gracefully
